tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"test runtime\");\n        let (client, connection) = rt\n            .block_on(tokio_postgres::connect(url, NoTls))\n            .expect(\"test connection\");\n        rt.spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        (client, rt)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn postgres_roundtrip_when_available() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(mut s) = store_or_skip(\"postgres_roundtrip_when_available\") else {\n            return;\n        };\n\n        let col = CollectionId::new(\"tasks\");\n        let entity = Entity::new(\n            col.clone(),\n            EntityId::new(\"t-001\"),\n            serde_json::json!({\"title\": \"hello\"}),\n        );\n        s.put(entity).expect(\"test operation should succeed\");\n        let got = s\n            .get(&col, &EntityId::new(\"t-001\"))\n            .expect(\"test operation should succeed\")\n            .expect(\"test operation should succeed\");\n        assert_eq!(got.data[\"title\"], \"hello\");\n        assert_eq!(got.version, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn unregister_collection_cleans_up_legacy_collection_views_when_available() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(database) = database_or_skip(\n            \"unregister_collection_cleans_up_legacy_collection_views_when_available\",\n        ) else {\n            return;\n        };\n\n        let (legacy_client, legacy_rt) = connect_raw_for_test(database.url());\n        legacy_rt\n            .block_on(async {\n                legacy_client\n                    .batch_execute(\n                        \"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS collection_views;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS collections;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS namespaces;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS databases;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS entities;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS schemas;\n                         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS audit_log;\",\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                legacy_client\n                    .batch_execute(\n                        \"CREATE TABLE collections (\n                            name TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY\n                        );\n                        CREATE TABLE collection_views (\n                            collection    TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,\n                            version       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n                            view_json     JSONB NOT NULL,\n                            updated_at_ns BIGINT NOT NULL,\n                            updated_by    TEXT\n                        );\",\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                Ok::<_, tokio_postgres::Error>(())\n            })\n            .expect(\"legacy schema should be created\");\n\n        let collection = CollectionId::new(\"ephemeral\");\n        let legacy_view = CollectionView {\n            collection: collection.clone(),\n            description: Some(\"legacy view\".into()),\n            markdown_template: \"# {{title}}\".into(),\n            version: 1,\n            updated_at_ns: Some(42),\n            updated_by: Some(\"legacy\".into()),\n        };\n        let legacy_view_json =\n            serde_json::to_value(&legacy_view).expect(\"legacy collection view should serialize\");\n\n        legacy_rt\n            .block_on(async {\n                legacy_client\n                    .execute(\n                        \"INSERT INTO collections (name) VALUES ($1)\",\n                        &[&collection.as_str()],\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                legacy_client\n                    .execute(\n                        \"INSERT INTO collection_views (collection, version, view_json, updated_at_ns, updated_by)\n                         VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)\",\n                        &[\n                            &collection.as_str(),\n                            &1_i32,\n                            &legacy_view_json,\n                            &42_i64,\n                            &Some(\"legacy\"),\n                        ],\n                    )\n                    .await?;\n                Ok::<_, tokio_postgres::Error>(())\n            })\n            .expect(\"legacy data should insert\");\n        drop(legacy_client);\n        drop(legacy_rt);\n\n        let mut adapter = PostgresStorageAdapter::connect(database.url())\n            .expect(\"adapter should connect after upgrade\");\n        assert!(\n            adapter\n                .get_collection_view(&collection)\n                .expect(\"legacy view should be readable after upgrade\")\n                .is_some(),\n            \"upgraded adapter should observe the pre-fix collection view\"\n        );\n\n        adapter\n            .unregister_collection(&collection)\n            .expect(\"unregister_collection should succeed on upgraded database\");\n\n        assert!(\n            adapter\n                .get_collection_view(&collection)\n                .expect(\"collection view lookup should succeed after unregister\")\n                .is_none(),\n            \"legacy collection view should be removed during unregister\"\n        );\n\n        let remaining_views: i64 = adapter\n            .block(adapter.client.query_one(\n                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM collection_views WHERE collection = $1\",\n                &[&collection.as_str()],\n            ))\n            .expect(\"count query should succeed\")\n            .get(0);\n        assert_eq!(\n            remaining_views, 0,\n            \"stale collection view rows must be deleted\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn namespace_catalogs_allow_same_name_without_cross_drop() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(mut s) = store_or_skip(\"namespace_catalogs_allow_same_name_without_cross_drop\")\n        else {\n            return;\n        };\n        let invoices = CollectionId::new(\"invoices\");\n        let billing = Namespace::new(\"prod\", \"billing\");\n        let engineering = Namespace::new(\"prod\", \"engineering\");\n\n        s.create_database(\"prod\")\n            .expect(\"database create should succeed\");\n        s.create_namespace(&billing)\n            .expect(\"billing namespace create should succeed\");\n        s.create_namespace(&engineering)\n            .expect(\"engineering namespace create should succeed\");\n\n        s.register_collection_in_namespace(&invoices, &Namespace::default_ns())\n            .expect(\"default collection register should succeed\");\n        s.register_collection_in_namespace(&invoices, &billing)\n            .expect(\"billing collection register should succeed\");\n        s.register_collection_in_namespace(&invoices, &engineering)\n            .expect(\"engineering collection register should succeed\");\n\n        assert_eq!(\n            s.list_namespace_collections(&billing)\n                .expect(\"billing list should succeed\"),\n            vec![invoices.clone()]\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            s.list_namespace_collections(&engineering)\n                .expect(\"engineering list should succeed\"),\n            vec![invoices.clone()]\n        );\n\n        s.drop_namespace(&billing)\n            .expect(\"billing drop should succeed\");\n        assert_eq!(\n            s.list_namespace_collections(&engineering)\n                .expect(\"engineering list should survive billing drop\"),\n            vec![invoices.clone()]\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            s.list_namespace_collections(&Namespace::default_ns())\n                .expect(\"default list should survive billing drop\"),\n            vec![invoices.clone()]\n        );\n\n JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! # Verification order\n//!\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer <token>` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\n//! 2. Parse + verify HS256 signature → `CredentialInvalid` / `CredentialMalformed`.\n//! 3. Check `iss` against issuer ID → `CredentialForeignIssuer`.\n//! 4. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\n//!\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::extract::State;\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\n\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\npub struct JwtIssuer {\n    secret: Vec<u8>,\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\n    pub issuer_id: String,\n}\n\nimpl JwtIssuer {\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec<u8>, issuer_id: String) -> Self {\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\n    }\n\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\n    pub fn issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError> {\n        encode(\n            &Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\n            claims,\n            &EncodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret),\n        )\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\n    }\n\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\n    ///\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\n    pub fn verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError> {\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\n Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n//!\n//! # Authentication\n//!\n//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n//!\n//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n//! |------|------|-------|------|\n//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n//!\n//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n//!\n//! ACL tag → role mapping:\n//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\n//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\n//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\n//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n//!\n//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n//!\n//! # Request flow\n//!\n//! ```text\n//! TCP accept\n//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\n//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\n//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\n//!             └─ route handler\n//!                  ├─ extract Extension<Identity>\n//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n//!                  └─ handler logic\n//! ```\n//!\n//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\npub mod actor_scope;\npub mod auth;\nmod collection_listing;\npub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\npub mod control_plane;\npub mod control_plane_routes;\npub mod cors_config;\npub mod user_roles;\npub mod gateway;\npub mod idempotency;\npub(crate) mod mcp_http;\npub mod mcp_stdio;\npub mod rate_limit;\npub mod schema_registry;\npub mod serve;\npub mod service;\npub mod tenant_router;\n\npub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\npub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;\npub use service::AxonServiceImpl;\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":43,"oldLines":6,"newStart":43,"newLines":7,"lines":[" //! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`]."," pub mod actor_scope;"," pub mod auth;","+pub mod auth_pipeline;"," mod collection_listing;"," pub(crate) mod embedded_ui;"," pub mod control_plane;"]},{"oldStart":60,"oldLines":5,"newStart":61,"newLines":6,"lines":[" pub mod tenant_router;"," "," pub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};","+pub use auth_pipeline::{InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};"," pub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;"," pub use service::AxonServiceImpl;"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01CK6wwgEq2n1LP4QVdfHsPL","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01G11hf2mEssXRvin3Et23Gc","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs","content":"//! Unit tests for the JWT auth pipeline (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Covers:\n//! - Every row of the failure-mode table (14 variants)\n//! - JWT round-trip\n//! - ResolvedIdentity installation in request extensions\n//! - Revocation cache invalidation\n//! - Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    struct ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: String,\n        sub: String,\n        aud: Vec<String>,\n        jti: String,\n        iat: u64,\n        nbf: u64,\n        exp: u64,\n        grants: Grants,\n    }\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: vec![TENANT.to_string()], // array, not string\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\n            }],\n        },\n    };\n    let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n        &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n        &bad_claims,\n        &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n    )\n    .unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 3: Signature invalid → 401 credential_invalid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_signature_invalid() {\n    let wrong_issuer = JwtIssuer::new(b\"wrong-secret\".to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = wrong_issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_invalid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 4: `exp` in past beyond skew → 401 credential_expired\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_exp_expired() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.exp = now - 31; // 31 seconds in the past, beyond the 30s skew\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_expired\");\n}\n\n/// Row 5: `nbf` in future beyond skew → 401 credential_not_yet_valid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_nbf_future() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.nbf = now + 31; // 31 seconds from now, beyond the 30s skew\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_not_yet_valid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 6: `jti` revoked → 401 credential_revoked\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jti_revoked() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims.jti.parse().unwrap();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    let state = make_state();\n    // Insert the JTI directly into the revocation cache.\n    state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_revoked\");\n}\n\n/// Row 7: `iss` unknown → 401 credential_foreign_issuer\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_foreign_issuer() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.iss = \"some-other-issuer\".to_string();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_foreign_issuer\");\n}\n\n/// Row 8: `aud` ≠ URL tenant → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_wrong_tenant() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.aud = \"other-tenant\".to_string(); // URL says \"acme\"\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    struct ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: String,\n        sub: String,\n        aud: Vec<String>,\n        jti: String,\n        iat: u64,\n        nbf: u64,\n        exp: u64,\n        grants: Grants,\n    }\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: vec![TENANT.to_string()], // array, not string\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\n            }],\n        },\n    };\n    let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n        &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n        &bad_claims,\n        &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n    )\n    .unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01BcRLMXUs4TZUS5Hhv4m4p8","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01QqPQtyiL6uPht7wAo243yE","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs","content":"//! Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\n//!\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\nconst ISSUER: &str = \"integration-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"u-integration-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"integration-test-secret\";\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Integration User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service should not fail\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn bearer(token: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\"Bearer {token}\")\n}\n\nfn valid_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants,\n    };\n    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\")\n}\n\nfn orders_read_grants() -> Grants {\n    Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"unauthenticated\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200 ──────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: \"different-tenant\".to_string(), // URL says \"acme\"\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\n    };\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"credential_wrong_tenant\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 database_not_granted ──────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_database_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"invoices\".to_string(), // URL says \"orders\"\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, grants);\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"database_not_granted\"\n    );\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":575,"cache_read_input_tokens":132304,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":575},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"cc1c03fa-01b8-4915-97d1-edc4d799b3f6"}

The axum handler serves them at `/ui/*path` with correct `Content-Type`\n//! headers, and falls back to `index.html` for any unrecognised path so that\n//! SvelteKit client-side routing works correctly.\n//!\n//! **Build prerequisite**: `ui/build` must exist before `cargo build`.\n//! Run `cd ui && bun run build` (or `npm run build`) first.\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::{header, StatusCode, Uri};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse rust_embed::RustEmbed;\n\n/// All files under `ui/build`, embedded at compile time.\n///\n/// The `#[folder]` path is relative to the crate root (`crates/axon-server/`),\n/// so `../../ui/build` resolves to `ui/build` in the workspace root.\n#[derive(RustEmbed)]\n#[folder = \"../../ui/build\"]\nstruct UiAssets;\n\n/// Axum handler for `GET /ui` and `GET /ui/*path`.\n///\n/// Strips the `/ui` prefix, looks up the file in [`UiAssets`], and responds\n/// with the correct `Content-Type`.  Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\n/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\npub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -> Response {\n    let raw = uri.path();\n    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\n    let asset_path = raw\n        .strip_prefix(\"/ui/\")\n        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\"/ui\"))\n        .unwrap_or(raw);\n    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\n        \"index.html\"\n    } else {\n        asset_path\n    };\n\n    serve_asset(asset_path)\n}\n\nfn serve_asset(path: &str) -> Response {\n    match UiAssets::get(path) {\n        Some(content) => {\n            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\n            Response::builder()\n                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\n                .body(Body::from(content.data))\n                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\n        }\n        None => {\n            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\n            match UiAssets::get(\"index.html\") {\n                Some(index) => Response::builder()\n                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"text/html; charset=utf-8\")\n                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\n                None => StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":66,"startLine":1,"totalLines":66}}}

          databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No ops_matrix ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn ops_matrix() {\n    use http::Method;\n\n    struct Case {\n        method: Method,\n        ops: Vec<Op>,\n        expected_status: u16,\n    }\n    let cases = vec![\n        // GET (Read) against various grant sets\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        // Write methods (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) against various grant sets\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PUT, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PUT, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::PATCH, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PATCH, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n    ];\n\n    // Use a route that accepts all HTTP methods.\n    let handler = || async { StatusCode::OK };\n\n    for case in cases {\n        let issuer_inner = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n        let mut claims = valid_claims();\n        claims.grants = Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: case.ops,\n            }],\n        };\n        let token = issuer_inner.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n        let state = make_state();\n        // Router that handles any method on TEST_PATH.\n        let app = Router::new()\n            .route(\n                TEST_PATH,\n                axum::routing::any(handler),\n            )\n            .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n                state,\n                jwt_verify_layer,\n            ));\n\n        let req = Request::builder()\n            .method(case.method.clone())\n            .uri(TEST_PATH)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n            .body(Body::empty())\n            .unwrap();\n        let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            resp.status().as_u16(),\n            case.expected_status,\n            \"method={} expected {}\",\n            case.method,\n            case.expected_status\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n// ── error_code / status_code consistency ──────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn auth_error_status_and_code_coverage() {\n    // Verify every variant maps to the correct status and code string.\n    let cases: &[(AuthError, u16, &str)] = &[\n        (AuthError::Unauthenticated, 401, \"unauthenticated\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialMalformed, 401, \"credential_malformed\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialInvalid, 401, \"credential_invalid\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialExpired, 401, \"credential_expired\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid, 401, \"credential_not_yet_valid\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialRevoked, 401, \"credential_revoked\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer, 401, \"credential_foreign_issuer\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant, 403, \"credential_wrong_tenant\"),\n        (AuthError::UserSuspended, 401, \"user_suspended\"),\n        (AuthError::NotATenantMember, 403, \"not_a_tenant_member\"),\n        (AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted, 403, \"database_not_granted\"),\n        (AuthError::OpNotGranted, 403, \"op_not_granted\"),\n        (AuthError::GrantsExceedIssuerRole, 401, \"grants_exceed_issuer_role\"),\n        (AuthError::GrantsMalformed, 401, \"grants_malformed\"),\n    ];\n    for (err, expected_status, expected_code) in cases {\n        assert_eq!(\n            err.status_code(),\n            *expected_status,\n            \"status mismatch for {:?}\",\n            err\n        );\n ───────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn revocation_via_storage_fallback() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims.jti.parse().unwrap();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    // Build storage with the JTI revoked at the storage level.\n    let mut {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\n\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\n\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\n\n## When to Use\n\n- After implementing a tracked issue\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\n\n## What It Does\n\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\n2. **Format** commit message:\n   - First line: `<issue-id> <concise summary>` (under 72 chars)\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\n\n## Commit Message Format\n\n```\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\n\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\n\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\n```\n\n## Examples\n\n```bash\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z","isSynthetic":true}

data.text: The generic auth search confirmed there are multiple auth surfaces, not one obvious bug. I’m narrowing to the server startup auth-key path first because it’s the most direct “auth bug” candidate and already has a dedicated helper.

cli/cmd/server.go:13:// resolveTsnetAuthKey applies auth key precedence:
cli/cmd/server.go:14:// TS_AUTHKEY env var > --tsnet-auth-key CLI flag > config file auth_key.

data.text: There’s a dedicated auth-key helper test, so that path is probably already covered. I’m checking the server trust gate next, because that’s where 403/401 behavior is enforced and where a subtle auth regression would usually land.

The code path most likely to hide an auth regression is the trusted-transport gate. I’m running the narrow auth tests first so I can tell whether this is a real bug in the current tree or just a vague bead title.
The tests are still running. While they do, I’m checking the exact trusted-request implementation and the server-facing auth tests for a mismatch between intended behavior and actual coverage.

data.text: The server-side auth gates are green, so the bug is probably not in the trust middleware. I’m polling the command-level auth-key test now and then I’ll branch to the next plausible auth surface if that stays clean too.

The existing tests don’t reproduce a failure, so I’m mining history and nearby tests for the exact auth behavior that changed. If there’s a latent regression, it should show up as a recent commit or an untested edge case.

data.text: The auth surfaces themselves look consistent, so I’m checking the last local commit and the current branch delta for accidental regressions. A bug bead like this often corresponds to a recent edit that changed behavior outside the obvious auth functions.

data.output:
commit 6c4b42d6d469b13cbb4bf06681f35ffe4d64c7eb
Author: Erik LaBianca <erik.labianca@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Jun 30 00:14:06 2026 -0400

    agent: detect reparented DDx attempt descendants by cwd [ddx-54d9c455]
    
    Broaden procExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner to recognize DDx-owned
    descendants whose cwd sits under the execution worktree root, even when the
    cwd no longer carries an explicit .execute-bead-wt-* suffix. This covers the
    reparented codex/claude orphans observed in production whose original parent
    died and left exo-open/auth-callback children stranded under ~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.
    
    Preserves the existing .execute-bead-wt-* classification and live-worker
    exemptions; adds the broader path-prefix check as a fallback.
    
    Test TestStaleAttemptProcessScanner_DetectsReparentedDescendantsByCwd asserts
    a ppid=1 process with cwd under .cache/ddx/exec-wt/ is classified DDx-owned.

data.text: The narrow tests passed, so I’m widening just enough to catch a hidden regression in the auth-adjacent packages. If there’s a bug here, a fuller `cmd` or `server` run should surface it without me guessing at the fix.

While the suites run, I’m checking the recent auth/error-handling fixes in history. That’s the best clue for what the current “auth bug” bead might be pointing at if the obvious trust gates are already green.

commit 702fbb75467838e73c01df526d77ca2977ca48a7
Author: Erik LaBianca <erik.labianca@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Apr 17 23:03:44 2026 -0400

    fix: surface auth/rate-limit errors in ddx agent run exit message [ddx-13a51971]
    
    Previously, agent failures from auth errors (401, token expired, rate
    limit) showed only "agent exited with code -1" with no diagnostic. Now
    the error message and up to 5 stderr lines are included so operators
    can see what went wrong and take action (e.g. re-authenticate).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit 87472cfc708148de7a9b1c3e51383cd366f7fd94
Author: Test User <test@ddx.test>
Date:   Wed Apr 15 07:57:52 2026 -0400

    security(server): gate /graphql on isTrusted() [final-review-fix]
    
    Final opus review of the Svelte migration epic (ddx-02d6142d) caught a
    must-fix auth bypass: handleGraphQLQuery at cli/internal/server/server.go:3539
    did not call isTrusted(r). Every /api/* handler gates on isTrusted;
    /graphql did not. Default bind is 127.0.0.1 so risk is local-process
    only, but any operator running `ddx server --addr 0.0.0.0` shipped an
    unauthenticated GraphQL endpoint serving bead data, worker logs,
    mutations, and subscriptions. The epic explicitly scoped "isTrusted()
    is still the gatekeeper; no auth bypass introduced" — this was the
    last gap.
    
    One-line fix: add the standard isTrusted + 403 preamble at the top of
    handleGraphQLQuery, mirroring every other gated handler.
    
    Test fixture update: 13 graphql test request sites across 7 files
    needed req.RemoteAddr = "127.0.0.1:12345" to simulate a loopback peer
    (httptest.NewRequest default RemoteAddr 192.0.2.1:1234 is non-loopback).
    Same pattern used by existing server_test.go /api/* tests.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

data.text: The repo has one more plausible auth surface: repository connection configuration. I’m checking that test because it uses auth-related config keys that may expose a mismatch between accepted config and what the command actually persists.
I’m checking whether the auth key actually reaches the ts-net config path, not just the helper test. A helper can pass while startup still drops the value if the config object is overwritten later.

data.text: I’m checking the operator-prompt identity code next. It’s another auth boundary, and the comments there may reveal a missing condition that the current tests don’t exercise.

I’m correlating the auth code with the recent security commits now. If a bug was recently fixed elsewhere, the surrounding commit messages usually reveal the adjacent surface that still needs cleanup.

The same HTTP mux serves both localhost and ts-net listeners. The localhost
guard on dispatch endpoints (`isLocalhost()`) should be extended to also
accept ts-net-identified requests.

### Configuration Surface

| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
|-----|------|---------|---------|
| `server.tsnet.enabled` | bool | false | Enable ts-net listener |
| `server.tsnet.hostname` | string | "ddx" | Tailscale hostname |
| `server.tsnet.auth_key` | string | "" | Tailscale auth key (for headless/CI); see precedence note below |
| `server.tsnet.state_dir` | string | ".ddx/tsnet" | ts-net state directory |
| `TS_AUTHKEY` | env var | unset | Tailscale auth key (preferred; avoids secrets in config files or shell history) |

**Auth key precedence:** `TS_AUTHKEY` env var > `--tsnet-auth-key` CLI flag > `server.tsnet.auth_key` config field. Prefer `TS_AUTHKEY` in CI and headless environments — secrets on the CLI are visible in `ps` output and shell history.

### What This Replaces

- The `auth` package (`cli/internal/auth/`) was designed for git platform
  authentication (GitHub, GitLab tokens). It remains for that purpose.
- Server network authentication is handled entirely by ts-net at the
  transport layer. No application-level auth middleware is needed.
- The MCP bead write tools (ddx_bead_create, ddx_bead_update, ddx_bead_claim)
  previously flagged as needing "optional API key auth" now get auth from
  ts-net instead.
- Identity policy is future work over the identity envelope, not a replacement
  for the current ts-net/localhost trust boundary.

## Consequences

- **Zero-config for tailnet users**: Anyone on the same tailnet can access
  the DDx server by hostname. No API keys to distribute.
- **No key management**: No generation, rotation, or revocation of API keys.
  Identity comes from Tailscale.
- **Tailscale dependency**: Users who want remote access must have Tailscale
  installed. This is acceptable for a developer tool — Tailscale is widely
  adopted in dev teams.
- **Localhost remains free**: Local-only users are unaffected. ts-net is
  opt-in.
- **~5MB binary size increase**: The tsnet library adds to the Go binary.

## ddx server

Start the DDx HTTP and MCP server

### Synopsis

Start the DDx server exposing documents, beads, document graph,
and agent session logs over HTTP REST and MCP endpoints.

HTTP API:
  GET /api/health              Liveness check
  GET /api/ready               Readiness check with dependency status
  GET /api/documents           List library documents (?type=)

  GET /api/beads/status        Bead summary counts
  GET /api/beads/dep/tree/:id  Dependency tree for a bead
  GET /api/docs/graph          Document dependency graph

  GET /api/docs/:id            Document metadata and staleness

  GET /api/docs/:id/dependents Downstream dependents

      --addr string                   Address to bind to (default "127.0.0.1")

data.text: I’m also checking the forwarded-request gate, because that’s the one place where a local trust check is combined with attestation headers. If there’s an auth bypass, it would likely be there rather than in the simple loopback gate.

I’m checking the legacy `internal/auth` package too. The server auth path is healthy, so if this bead is really about an auth regression, the remaining candidate is the git-platform auth helper that the server docs explicitly say still exists.

The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a. 2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in: {"type":"compaction.start",...} {"type":"compaction.end",...,"data":{"messages_after":249,"messages_before":249,"no_compaction":true,"success":false}} {"type":"compaction.start",...} {"type":"compaction.end",...,"data":{"messages_after":249,"messages_before":249,"no_compaction":true,"success":false}} ... 3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work. 4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same runner/route-target and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it. ## Expected - After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate - Compaction should either succeed (messages_after < messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely - If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin ## Scope - Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status - Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn - Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls - Add a watchdog at the work level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget ## Evidence - Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB) - Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master - Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed) ## Acceptance - [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally - [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic - [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome - [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:stability"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-25a41f51","title":"bug: embedded agent stalls in post-commit compaction loop","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"The embedded 'agent' runner can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. Downstream consumer DDx ran into this mid-session on a production worker: ~8461 stuck-compaction events accumulated before the hosting process had to be killed. ## Evidence DDx-side session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB) Parent process killed at PID 4141811 after wedging for hours on a completed task. ## DDx-side workaround (defense in depth, does not fix the root cause) Commit 604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0 in ddx repo (bead ddx-25a41f51) wraps the embedded agent call with two guards: 1. compaction.start and compaction.end(no_compaction=true) events no longer pulse the caller's inactivity timer — only real-progress events (LLM calls, tool results, commits) extend the timeout. 2. Circuit breaker counts consecutive compaction.end(success=false, no_compaction=true) events and aborts with a structured compaction_stuck error after a configurable threshold (default 50). The workaround catches the stuck state at the DDx/caller layer. The native fix belongs in github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent so every ddx-agent user (not just DDx CLI) benefits. ## Requested upstream fix - Compaction driver detects its own stuck state via the same heuristic (consecutive success=false/no_compaction=true events) and returns a structured error to the caller - Eliminates the need for the caller to interpret compaction events as activity signals - Exposes a clean API signal so consumers can decide whether to retry, escalate, or abort - Regression test seeds the stuck state and verifies the driver bails instead of looping ## Scope - Bounded attempt counter inside the compaction loop (not just the caller) - Structured error: compaction.ErrStuck or similar - Integration test using a deterministic seed that reproduces the 33MB jsonl pattern ## Relationship to DDx When this lands upstream, DDx can remove its circuit-breaker workaround and rely on the native error. Until then, DDx keeps both layers as belt-and-suspenders.","acceptance":"(1) Compaction loop bounded with a structural stop. (2) Native structured error signals stuck state to callers. (3) Regression test reproduces the stuck state via seeded read-only tool calls. Evidence includes the generated execution bundle `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, worktree `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, and the current work implementation in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`. `ddx agent work` selected and launched an open `epic` bead from the HELIX execution queue instead of a bounded executable bead. ## Reproduction 1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx bead ready --json --execution`. 2. Observe the first candidate is `helix-fef22846`, `issue_type=epic`, title `Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement`. 3. Run `ddx agent work --once --json`. 4. Observe DDx creates `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7` and `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json` with `bead_id=helix-fef22846`. ## Observed gap `ReadyExecution()` currently treats any open, unblocked bead as execution-ready unless `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, or cooldown metadata says otherwise. `work` then takes the first candidate without a stronger structural execution screen. That behavior is weaker than the API-001 execute-bead supervisor contract, which says the loop should run a generic execution-ready validator against ordered candidates before launch. ## Why this matters Open epics represent umbrella planning/decomposition work, not necessarily a bounded workspace transform. The WorkerManager in cli/internal/server/workers.go already tracks this (WorkerRecord.CurrentBead at line 51, WorkerRecord.State, WorkerRecord.Runner, WorkerRecord.Route-target, WorkerRecord.Attempts/Successes/Failures) but it is only accessible through the server API. Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all. This bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool. ## Required changes ### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go) Register alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags: --json emit raw JSON array --watch re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C --project filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project) ### 2. Data sources (two paths) A. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler. B. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by: 1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees) 2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, runner, route-target, started_at ### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan For each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*: - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix) - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain) - Read work-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields ### 4. Table output (default) Columns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | RUNNER | ROUTE-TARGET | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS - STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red) - ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s) - If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID ### 5. Merge and deduplicate When both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker. ## Key files - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand() - cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771) - cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)","acceptance":"ddx agent workers prints a table with STATE, BEAD ID, TITLE, RUNNER, ROUTE-TARGET, ELAPSED, ATTEMPTS columns. iteration commits land only execution evidence","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. Result: iteration commit is built by ddx-land-coordinator with ONLY execution-evidence files (.ddx/executions//result.json, manifest.json, usage.json), no actual code or doc changes. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'. Concrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29): Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead) Runner: claude / route-target: sonnet Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts, ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files — .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines) .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines) Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of route-target time spent making them. The same pattern appeared on: 20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK) 20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled) 20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK) 20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK) Total burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed. The execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit: '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add ...' So agent self-commit is the contract. But: (a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the runner sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence. (b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect. The issue spans multiple runneres (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one upstream-service's commit semantics. Either: - The runner should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language - The runner should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as runner-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","acceptance":"AC1. When an execute-bead session exits without committing in the worktree AND without writing .ddx/executions//no_changes_rationale.txt, the runner records a NEW dedicated status (e.g. 'agent_silent_exit' or 'no_evidence_produced') instead of letting ddx-land-coordinator land an iteration containing only execution evidence. AC2. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the runner catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost. AC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'. AC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the runner logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure. AC5.
