./cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/[workerId]/+page.svelte:185: return `/executions/${encodeURIComponent(workerId)}/result.json`; ./cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":" auth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension Implement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server. ## What you're building An axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely. ## Scope — exactly what to add ### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs` A module exposing two things: 1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result` and a `verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result` for round-tripping in tests. 2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent. The middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock>` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss. ### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` - Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`. - Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(&self) -> u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(&self) -> &'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.) - Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&GrantedDatabase>` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(&self, op: Op) -> bool`. ### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait) Add one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait: ```rust async fn is_jti_revoked(&self, jti: Uuid) -> Result; ``` Implement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it. ### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs` Re-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation. ## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4) 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer ` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2. 2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it). 3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401). 4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks. 5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)>` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware. 6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -> Result, AxonError>` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401). 7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -> Result, AxonError>`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403). 8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification. 9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`. ## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4 The mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core. | Failure | Status | `error.code` | |---|---|---| | No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` | | Header not `Bearer ` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` | | JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` | | `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` | | Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` | | `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` | | `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` | | `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` | | `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` | | `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` | | `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` | | `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` | | URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` | | Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` | Note the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403. ## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete ### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs` For every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases. Additionally: - `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match. - `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions. - `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly. - `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`. - `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome. ### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs` Build a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with: - No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated` - Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: "orders", ops: [read]}]` → 200 - Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant` - Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted` ### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement` **If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload. ## Crate dependencies you will need Add to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`: ```toml jsonwebtoken = "9" ``` Everything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace. ## Context you must know about ### A1 landed: schema - `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc. - `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected. - Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`. ### A2 landed: types - `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants). - `parse_claims(&str) -> Result` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT. - `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match. - Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc. ### Files you should NOT touch - Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files. - The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module. ## Quality bar You are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher power class. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued. Specifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. Fix the issues first. - [ ] crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs exists and compiles - [ ] JwtIssuer can sign and verify a JwtClaims round-trip (HS256) - [ ] Middleware implements the 8-step verification order - [ ] Every row of the ADR-018 §4 failure table has a matching unit test asserting (status, error_code) - [ ] Revocation cache invalidation test passes without sleep - [ ] Clock skew test passes at 30s boundary (both directions) - [ ] Integration test with a dummy protected route covers the 4 golden paths - [ ] is_jti_revoked wired into memory/sqlite/postgres storage adapters (trait + 3 impls) - [ ] get_user and get_tenant_member wired similarly - [ ] ResolvedIdentity lives in axon-core and is installed into the axum request extension - [ ] cargo test -p axon-server green - [ ] cargo test -p axon-storage green (new trait methods added) - [ ] cargo test -p axon-core green (new helpers added) - [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean helix, area:auth, spec:ADR-018, phase:middleware Sections 4 (Credentials) and 4 Verification Order — normative for everything in this bead Credentials (JWT, V5) and Identity (Authentication) sections You are a coding agent implementing a single bead inside an isolated git worktree. Work from the bead description above — it is complete and unambiguous. The AC list is the completion contract. ## How to work 1. **Read first, act second.** Read the governing ADR-018 sections and the existing crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs so you understand the AuthError variants and the parse_claims wrapper. Then read crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs to confirm the credential_revocations table shape. Then start coding. 2. **Cross-reference your work against the AC as you go.** Every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a piece of code you can point to. Before committing, re-read the AC and physically tick each one. 3. **Run the verification commands locally before committing.** cargo test and cargo clippy per the AC. If either fails, fix it. Do not commit red code. 4. **Commit once, when everything works.** Do not commit partial or exploratory changes. The bead is a single atomic unit of work. 5. **If you cannot complete the bead**, write `.ddx/executions/b1-enhanced-manual/no_changes_rationale.txt` with (a) what's done, (b) what's blocking, (c) why a follow-up bead is needed. Do not commit red code to "make progress". ## Constraints - Work only in the files listed under "Scope" and "Files you should NOT touch" above. - Do not modify CLAUDE.md or add new documentation. - Do not run `ddx init`. - Keep the `.ddx/executions/` directory intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence. - Commit with `git add && git commit -m 'feat(auth-pipeline): ... [axon-dccbdf73]'`. Do not `git add -A` — there may be other WIP in the worktree. ## Done definition `cargo test -p axon-server -p axon-core -p axon-storage` green + `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean + every AC item ticked + one commit. That's done. SQL migrations for auth/tenancy tables (FEAT-014, ADR-018).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Adds tables for:\n4\t//! - tenants: global account boundary\n5\t//! - users: global user identities\n6\t//! - user_identities: federated identity mapping\n7\t//! - tenant_users: M:N tenant membership\n8\t//! - tenant_databases: tenant-to-database mapping\n9\t//! - credential_revocations: revoked JWTs\n10\t//!\n11\t//! Note: `audit_retention_policies` is intentionally NOT included here — it\n12\t//! belongs to F1 audit-attribution (a separate bead / feature).\n13\t\n14\t/// Apply auth/tenancy migrations to a SQLite connection.\n15\t///\n16\t/// This is idempotent — running it multiple times on the same database\n17\t/// produces the same result without errors or duplicate rows.\n18\tpub fn apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> Result<(), String> {\n19\t // Create tenants table.\n20\t // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\n21\t conn.execute_batch(\n22\t \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\n23\t id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n24\t name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n25\t display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n26\t created_at_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,\n27\t updated_at_ms INTEGER NOT NULL\n28\t )\",\n29\t )\n30\t .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenants table: {e}\"))?;\n31\t\n32\t // Create users table.\n33\t conn.execute_batch(\n34\t \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n35\t id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n36\t display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n37\t email TEXT,\n38\t created_at_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,\n39\t suspended_at_ms INTEGER\n40\t )\",\n41\t )\n42\t .map_err(|e| format!(\"users table: {e}\"))?;\n43\t\n44\t // Create user_identities table.\n45\t // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\n46\t conn.execute_batch(\n47\t \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\n48\t provider TEXT NOT NULL,\n49\t external_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n50\t user_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\n51\t created_at_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,\n52\t PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\n53\t )\",\n54\t )\n55\t .map_err(|e| format!(\"user_identities table: {e}\"))?;\n56\t\n57\t // Create tenant_users table.\n58\t // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required by ADR-018.\n59\t conn.execute_batch(\n60\t \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_users (\n61\t tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\n62\t user_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\n63\t role TEXT NOT NULL,\n64\t added_at_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,\n65\t PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id)\n66\t )\",\n67\t )\n68\t .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenant_users table: {e}\"))?;\n69\t\n70\t // Create tenant_databases table.\n71\t conn.execute_batch(\n72\t \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_databases (\n73\t tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\n74\t database_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n75\t created_at_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,\n76\t PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, database_name)\n77\t )\",\n78\t )\n79\t .map_err(|e| Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! # Authentication\n4\t//!\n5\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n6\t//! injected as a typed extension. Three modes are supported:\n7\t//!\n8\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n9\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\n10\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n11\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n12\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role ` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n13\t//!\n14\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n15\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n16\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n17\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n18\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n19\t//!\n20\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\n21\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\n22\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\n23\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\n24\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n25\t//!\n26\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n27\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`. If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n28\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n29\t//!\n30\t//! # Request flow\n31\t//!\n32\t//! ```text\n33\t//! TCP accept\n34\t//! └─ authenticate_http_request (gateway.rs middleware)\n35\t//! ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer → Identity\n36\t//! └─ insert Identity into request extensions\n37\t//! └─ route handler\n38\t//! ├─ extract Extension\n39\t//! ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n40\t//! └─ handler logic\n41\t//! ```\n42\t//!\n43\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via Role-based and attribute-based access control for Axon (FEAT-012).\n1\t//!\n2\t//! # Layers\n3\t//!\n4\t//! **Layer 1 — RBAC** ([`Role`], [`CallerIdentity`], [`Operation`])\n5\t//!\n6\t//! Four built-in roles (`Admin > Write > Read > None`) control access to\n7\t//! Axon operations. Roles are derived from the identity provider (Tailscale\n8\t//! ACL tags, OIDC claims, etc.) by the server layer and passed into handlers\n9\t//! as a [`CallerIdentity`]. Handlers call [`CallerIdentity::check`] to\n10\t//! enforce the minimum required role.\n11\t//!\n12\t//! **Layer 2 — Field-level masking** ([`MaskPolicy`])\n13\t//!\n14\t//! A `MaskPolicy` hides individual entity fields from callers whose role\n15\t//! falls below a configured minimum. Applied by [`CallerIdentity::apply_masks`]\n16\t//! before returning entity data to the caller. Admins always see all fields.\n17\t//!\n18\t//! **Layer 3 — Collection write control** ([`WritePolicy`])\n19\t//!\n20\t//! A `WritePolicy` sets a per-collection minimum write role and marks fields\n21\t//! as immutable after creation. Checked by the handler before any mutation.\n22\t//!\n23\t//! **Layer 4 — Database-scoped grants** ([`DatabaseGrant`], [`GrantRegistry`])\n24\t//!\n25\t//! The grant registry\n28\t//! is stored in `__axon_policies__` and enforced at the routing layer.\n29\t\n30\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n31\t\n32\tuse crate::error::AxonError;\n33\t\n34\t/// Built-in roles for Axon RBAC (FEAT-012).\n35\t///\n36\t/// Roles are ordered by privilege: `Admin > Write > Read > None`.\n37\t/// When a user has multiple roles (e.g., from multiple ACL tags),\n38\t/// the highest-privilege role wins.\n39\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n40\tpub enum Role {\n41\t /// No access. Explicitly denied.\n42\t None = 0,\n43\t /// Read entities, query, traverse, browse audit log.\n44\t Read = 1,\n45\t /// Create, update, delete entities and links in any collection.\n46\t Write = 2,\n47\t /// All operations including drop and schema changes.\n48\t Admin = 3,\n49\t}\n50\t\n51\timpl Role {\n52\t /// Parse a role from a Tailscale ACL tag or string name.\n53\t ///\n54\t /// Accepted inputs (both full `tag:` prefix and bare name):\n55\t ///\n56\t /// | Input | Role |\n57\t /// |-------|------|\n58\t /// | `\"tag:axon-admin\"` / `\"tag:admin\"` / `\"admin\"` | `Admin` |\n59\t /// | `\"tag:axon-write\"` / `\"tag:axon-agent\"` / `\"tag:write\"` / `\"write\"` | `Write` |\n60\t /// | `\"tag:axon-read\"` / `\"tag:read\"` / `\"read\"` | `Read` |\n61\t /// | anything else | `None` |\n62\t ///\n63\t /// `tag:axon-agent` is an alias for `write` — the conventional tag for\n64\t /// automated agent workloads that need read/write but not admin access.\n65\t pub fn from_tag(tag: &str) -> Self {\n66\t match tag {\n67\t \"tag:axon-admin\" | \"tag:admin\" | \"admin\" => Role::Admin,\n68\t \"tag:axon-write\" | \"tag:axon-agent\" | \"tag:write\" | \"write\" => fn from_tags(tags: &[String]) -> Self {\n76\t tags.iter()\n77\t .map(|t| Role::from_tag(t))\n78\t .max()\n79\t .unwrap_or(Role::None)\n80\t }\n81\t}\n82\t\n83\timpl std::fmt::Display for Role {\n84\t fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n85\t match self {\n86\t Role::None => write!(f, \"none\"),\n87\t Role::Read => write!(f, \"read\"),\n88\t Role::Write => write!(f, \"write\"),\n89\t Role::Admin => write!(f, \"admin\"),\n90\t }\n91\t }\n92\t}\n93\t\n94\t/// The kind of operation being attempted.\n95\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n96\tpub enum namespaces.\n102\t Admin,\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\timpl Operation {\n106\t /// The minimum role required for this operation.\n107\t pub fn required_role(self) -> Role {\n108\t match self {\n109\t Operation::Read => Role::Read,\n110\t Operation::Write => Role::Write,\n111\t Operation::Admin => Role::Admin,\n112\t }\n113\t }\n114\t}\n115\t\n116\t/// A simple field-equality filter used as an agent scope constraint\n117\t/// (FEAT-022 / ADR-016).\n118\t///\n119\t/// When attached to a [`CallerIdentity`], the agent guardrails layer rejects\n120\t/// any mutation whose target entity data does not satisfy\n121\t/// `data[field] == value`. V1 supports only field-equality predicates;\n122\t/// compound and range filters are deferred.\n123\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n124\tpub struct EntityFilter {\n125\t /// The top-level data field to compare.\n126\t pub field: String,\n127\t /// The required value for that field.\n128\t pub value: serde_json::Value,\n129\t}\n130\t\n131\timpl EntityFilter {\n132\t /// Convenience constructor.\n133\t pub fn new(field: impl Into, value: serde_json::Value) -> Self {\n134\t Self {\n135\t field: field.into(),\n136\t value,\n137\t }\n138\t }\n139\t\n140\t /// Returns `true` when the JSON object `data` satisfies this filter.\n141\t ///\n142\t /// Returns `false` when `data` is not a JSON object, when the field is\n143\t /// absent, or when the value does not equal [`Self::value`].\n144\t pub fn matches(&self, data: &serde_json::Value) -> bool {\n145\t data.get(&self.field).is_some_and(|v| v == &self.value)\n146\t }\n147\t}\n148\t\n149\t/// Identity context for a request.\n150\t///\n151\t/// Populated by the auth middleware from the identity provider.\n152\t/// In `--no-auth` mode, all requests get `CallerIdentity::anonymous()`.\n153\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n154\tpub struct CallerIdentity {\n155\t /// Actor name for audit log entries.\n156\t pub actor: String,\n157\t /// The caller's effective role (highest privilege from all their tags).\n158\t pub role: Role,\n159\t /// Optional scope constraint enforced by the agent guardrails layer\n160\t /// (FEAT-022, ADR-016). modified.\n272\t pub fn check_immutable_fields(\n273\t &self,\n274\t old_data: &serde_json::Value,\n275\t new_data: &serde_json::Value,\n276\t ) -> Vec {\n277\t let mut violations = Vec::new();\n278\t prevent SQL injection in DDL.\n///\n/// PostgreSQL identifiers may contain letters, digits, `_`, and `$`, and\n/// must start with a letter or `_`. We apply a conservative subset of that\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n if name.is_empty() {\n return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\n \"database name must not be empty\".to_owned(),\n ));\n }\n let first = name.chars().next().expect(\"non-empty checked above\");\n if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() && first != '_' {\n return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n \"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\"\n )));\n }\n if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\n return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n \"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\"\n )));\n }\n Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\n/// `dbname` key.\n///\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database. We produce a\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: &str, tenant_db_name: &str) -> String {\n let target = format!(\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\");\n if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgres://\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgresql://\") {\n // URL format: replace path component.\n // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\n if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\"://\") {\n let after_scheme = &superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\n // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\n if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\n let scheme_authority = &superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\n let rest = &after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\n // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\n let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\n &rest[q..]\n } else {\n \"\"\n };\n return format!(\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\");\n }\n // No path: append the target database.\n return format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\");\n }\n format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\")\n } else {\n // Keyword–value format. Replace existing dbname= or append it.\n let mut parts: Vec = superadmin_dsn\n .split_whitespace()\n .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\"dbname=\"))\n .map(str::to_owned)\n .collect();\n parts.push(format!(\"dbname={target}\"));\n parts.join(\" \")\n }\n}\n\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\n/// superadmin connection.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\n///\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n .enable_all()\n .build()\n .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n rt.block_on(async {\n let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n .await\n .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n tokio::spawn(async move {\n let _ = connection.await;\n });\n let row = client\n .query_one(\n \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n &[&full_name.as_str()],\n )\n .await\n .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n if exists {\n return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\n \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n )));\n }\n // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\n // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\n client\n .simple_query(&format!(\"CREATE DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n .await\n .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n Ok(())\n })\n}\n\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::NotFound` if the database does not exist.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n .enable_all()\n .build()\n .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n rt.block_on(async {\n let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n .await\n .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n tokio::spawn(async move {\n let _ = connection.await;\n });\n let row = client\n .query_one(\n \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n &[&full_name.as_str()],\n )\n .await\n .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n if !exists {\n return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\n \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n )));\n }\n client\n .simple_query(&format!(\"DROP DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n .await\n .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n Ok(())\n })\n}\n\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\n// Explicit verification path:\n// AXON_TEST_POSTGRES=\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon_test\" cargo test -p axon-storage postgres::tests:: -- --nocapture\n// When JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! # Verification order\n//!\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer ` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\n//! 2. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\n//!\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::extract::State;\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\nuse Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n//!\n//! # Authentication\n//!\n//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n//! injected as a typed extension. Three modes are supported:\n//!\n//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n//! |------|------|-------|------|\n//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role ` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n//!\n//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n//!\n//! ACL tag → role mapping:\n//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\n//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\n//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\n//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n//!\n//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`. If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n//!\n//! # Request flow\n//!\n//! ```text\n//! TCP accept\n//! └─ authenticate_http_request (gateway.rs middleware)\n//! ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer → Identity\n//! └─ insert Identity into request extensions\n//! └─ route handler\n//! ├─ extract Extension\n//! ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n//! └─ handler logic\n//! ```\n//!\n//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\npub mod actor_scope;\npub mod auth;\nmod collection_listing;\npub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\npub mod control_plane;\npub mod control_plane_routes;\npub mod cors_config;\npub mod user_roles;\npub mod gateway;\npub mod idempotency;\npub(crate) mod mcp_http;\npub mod mcp_stdio;\npub mod rate_limit;\npub mod schema_registry;\npub mod serve;\npub mod service;\npub mod tenant_router;\n\npub use Unit tests for the JWT auth pipeline (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Covers:\n//! - Every row of the failure-mode table (14 variants)\n//! - JWT round-trip\n//! - ResolvedIdentity installation in request extensions\n//! - Revocation cache invalidation\n//! - Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n SystemTime::now()\n .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n .unwrap_or_default()\n .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n let now = now_secs();\n JwtClaims {\n iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n iat: now,\n nbf: now,\n exp: now + 3600,\n grants: Grants {\n databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n }],\n },\n }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n storage.insert_user(User {\n id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n email: None,\n created_at_ms: 0,\n suspended_at_ms: None,\n });\n storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n role: TenantRole::Write,\n });\n storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc {\n let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n let storage: Box =\n Box::new(make_storage());\n Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc) -> Router {\n Router::new()\n .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n state,\n jwt_verify_layer,\n ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n let status = resp.status();\n let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n .await\n .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request {\n Request::builder()\n .method(\"GET\")\n .uri(path)\n .body(Body::empty())\n .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request {\n Request::builder()\n .method(\"GET\")\n .uri(path)\n .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n .body(Body::empty())\n .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n let claims = valid_claims();\n let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n let state = make_state();\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n let state = make_state();\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = Request::builder()\n .uri(TEST_PATH)\n .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n .body(Body::empty())\n .unwrap();\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\n let state = make_state();\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n #[derive(Serialize)]\n struct ArrayAudClaims {\n iss: String,\n sub: String,\n aud: Vec,\n jti: String,\n iat: u64,\n nbf: u64,\n exp: u64,\n grants: Grants,\n }\n let now = now_secs();\n let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n aud: vec![TENANT.to_string()], // array, not string\n jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n iat: now,\n nbf: now,\n exp: now + 3600,\n grants: Grants {\n databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n ops: vec![Op::Read],\n }],\n },\n };\n let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n &bad_claims,\n &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n )\n .unwrap();\n let state = make_state();\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 3: Signature invalid → 401 credential_invalid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_signature_invalid() {\n let wrong_issuer = JwtIssuer::new(b\"wrong-secret\".to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n let claims = valid_claims();\n let token = wrong_issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n let state = make_state();\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_invalid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 4: `exp` in past beyond skew → 401 credential_expired\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_exp_expired() {\n let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n let now = now_secs();\n let mut claims = valid_claims();\n claims.exp = now - 31; // 31 seconds in the past, beyond the 30s skew\n let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n let state = make_state();\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_expired\");\n}\n\n/// Row 5: `nbf` in future beyond skew → 401 credential_not_yet_valid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_nbf_future() {\n let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n let now = now_secs();\n let mut claims = valid_claims();\n claims.nbf = now + 31; // 31 seconds from now, beyond the 30s skew\n let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n let state = make_state();\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_not_yet_valid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 6: `jti` revoked → 401 credential_revoked\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jti_revoked() {\n let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n let claims = valid_claims();\n let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims.jti.parse().unwrap();\n let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n let state = make_state();\n // Insert the JTI directly into the revocation cache.\n state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_revoked\");\n}\n\n/// Row 7: `iss` unknown → 401 credential_foreign_issuer\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_foreign_issuer() {\n let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n let mut claims = valid_claims();\n claims.iss = \"some-other-issuer\".to_string();\n let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n let state = make_state();\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_foreign_issuer\");\n}\n\n/// Row 8: `aud` ≠ URL tenant → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_wrong_tenant() {\n let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n let mut claims = valid_claims();\n claims.aud = \"other-tenant\".to_string(); // URL says \"acme\"\n let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n let state = make_state();\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_wrong_tenant\");\n}\n\n/// Row 9: `sub` suspended → 401 user_suspended\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_user_suspended() {\n let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n let claims = valid_claims();\n let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n // Build state with a suspended user.\n let mut storage = make_storage();\n // Overwrite the user with a suspended version.\n storage.insert_user(User {\n id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n display_name: \"Suspended\".to_string(),\n email: None,\n created_at_ms: 0,\n suspended_at_ms: Some(1000),\n });\n let state = Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string())),\n revocation_cache: .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n state,\n jwt_verify_layer,\n ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n let status = resp.status();\n let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n .await\n .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request {\n Request::builder()\n .method(\"GET\")\n .uri(path)\n .body(Body::empty())\n .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request {\n Request::builder()\n .method(\"GET\")\n .uri(path)\n .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n .body(Body::empty())\n .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip }],\n },\n };\n let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n &bad_claims,\n &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n )\n .unwrap();\n let state = make_state();\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n make_app(state);\n let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_invalid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 4: `exp` in past beyond skew → 401 credential_expired\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_exp_expired() {\n let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_revoked\");\n}\n\n/// Row 7: `iss` unknown → 401 credential_foreign_issuer\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_foreign_issuer() {\n token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n let state = make_state();\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n created_at_ms: 0,\n suspended_at_ms: Some(1000),\n });\n let state = Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string())),\n revocation_cache: not_a_tenant_member\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_tenant_member() {\n let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n let claims = valid_claims();\n let token = expected_status: 200 },\n Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n ];\n\n // Use a route that accepts all HTTP methods.\n let handler = || async { StatusCode::OK };\n\n for case in cases {\n let issuer_inner = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n let mut claims = valid_claims();\n claims.grants = Grants {\n databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n ops: case.ops,\n }],\n };\n let token = issuer_inner.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n let state = make_state();\n // Router that handles any method on TEST_PATH.\n let Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\n//! GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\n//!\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\nconst ISSUER: &str = \"integration-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"u-integration-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"integration-test-secret\";\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n SystemTime::now()\n .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n .unwrap_or_default()\n .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc {\n let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n storage.insert_user(User {\n id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n display_name: \"Integration User\".to_string(),\n email: None,\n created_at_ms: 0,\n suspended_at_ms: None,\n });\n storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n role: TenantRole::Write,\n });\n Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\n revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n Box::new(storage) as Box\n )),\n })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc) -> Router {\n Router::new()\n .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n state,\n jwt_verify_layer,\n ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request) -> (StatusCode, TenantId::new(TENANT),\n user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n role: TenantRole::Write,\n });\n Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\n revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n Box::new(storage) as Box\n )),\n })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc) -> Router {\n Router::new()\n Unit tests for the JWT auth pipeline (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Covers:\n//! - Every row of the failure-mode table (14 variants)\n//! - JWT round-trip\n//! - ResolvedIdentity installation in request extensions\n//! - Revocation cache invalidation\n//! - Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n SystemTime::now()\n .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n .unwrap_or_default()\n .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n let now = now_secs();\n JwtClaims {\n iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n iat: now,\n nbf: now,\n exp: now + 3600,\n grants: Grants {\n databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n }],\n },\n }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n storage.insert_user(User {\n id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n email: None,\n created_at_ms: 0,\n suspended_at_ms: None,\n });\n storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n role: TenantRole::Write,\n });\n storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc {\n let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n let storage: Box =\n Box::new(make_storage());\n Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc) -> Router {\n Router::new()\n .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n state,\n jwt_verify_layer,\n ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n let status = resp.status();\n let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n .await\n .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request {\n Request::builder()\n .method(\"GET\")\n .uri(path)\n .body(Body::empty())\n .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request {\n Request::builder()\n .method(\"GET\")\n .uri(path)\n .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n .body(Body::empty())\n .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n let claims = valid_claims();\n let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n let state = make_state();\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n let state = make_state();\n let app = make_app(state);\n let req = Request::builder()\n .uri(TEST_PATH)\n .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n .body(Body::empty())\n .unwrap();\n let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\n\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\n\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\n\n## When to Use\n\n- After implementing a tracked issue\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\n\n## What It Does\n\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\n2. **Format** commit message:\n - First line: ` ` (under 72 chars)\n - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\n - Trailer: verification commands run\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\n - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\n - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\n - `npm test` for Node.js projects\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\n\n## Commit Message Format\n\n```\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\n\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\n\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\n```\n\n## Examples\n\n```bash\nhelix commit hx-abc123 # commit, gate, push, close issue\nhelix commit # commit and gate without tracker update\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z","isSynthetic":true} {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions. data.output:
# Execute-Bead Self-Commit Contract

`ddx try` runs an agent inside an isolated worktree. The agent must produce
exactly one of two durable signals before it exits:

- a git commit containing the completed implementation and tests
- `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt`

The rationale file is only for attempts that cannot produce a valid commit. It
must explain what is done, what is blocking, and what a follow-up attempt needs.
DDx treats that as a legitimate `no_changes` signal.

If the agent exits without a commit and without the rationale file, DDx records
`no_evidence_produced`. That status is a harness failure, not success and not
ordinary no-changes. DDx does not land an execution-evidence-only iteration, and
the reviewer does not need to spend a pass discovering that the diff contains
only `.ddx/executions` metadata.

When the worktree still has uncommitted paths at that point, DDx includes those
paths in `result.json` as `no_evidence_paths` so an operator can diagnose
whether the agent edited ignored files, failed to commit, or wrote outside the
intended scope.
